spec_match.rationale: Wires up sharedStorage.worklet.addModule + sharedStorage.run and the canonical interestGroups() call, but the worklet's console.log isn't surfaced into the page so visitors can't see the returned IG metadata shape (owner, name, timeSinceLastUpdateMs, lifetimeRemainingMs, etc.) — the headline payload of the feature.

interactive.rationale: Two real buttons: join an IG via navigator.joinAdInterestGroup and exercise sharedStorage.worklet.addModule + sharedStorage.run end-to-end, with a timestamped log per call.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises a baseline 'run the worklet that reads IGs' demo with aggregation framing; page delivers that probe + run + code listing.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers the no-API path with a probe and rejection log, but never demonstrates the empty-list case, the not-enrolled case, or the missing-permission-policy case — each surfaces a different error name the developer should recognise.

references.rationale: see-also lists chromestatus.com/feature/5074536530444288 alongside the WICG Shared Storage explainer.

design_system.rationale: All colour comes from CSS variables, accent-amber for the warn-block, and buttons inherit the global black/ivory rule without overriding background-only.

openQuestions.title: Surface the worklet's returned IG list into the page
openQuestions.detail: Right now the worklet only console.logs the array length, so visitors have no way to see the actual IG metadata (owner, name, timeSinceGroupJoinedMs, lifetimeRemainingMs, timeSinceLastUpdateMs, timeUntilNextUpdateMs, biddingLogicURL, etc.) that the new interestGroups() method returns. Pipe the worklet output back to the page via sharedStorage.set + sharedStorage.get, or via a MessageChannel, and render it as a table so the headline payload is visible.

openQuestions.title: Show the not-enrolled / no-permission-policy failure modes
openQuestions.detail: The API throws distinct errors for (a) missing chrome://flags, (b) origin not enrolled in Privacy Sandbox, (c) missing shared-storage permission policy header. The probe currently collapses all of these into 'sharedStorage not available' — split them out so developers know which knob to flip.

openQuestions.title: Add a non-cross-origin iframe variant
openQuestions.detail: interestGroups() behaves differently in a fenced frame vs a normal window context. Add a same-origin iframe (or a documented switch) that exercises both contexts so visitors see the surface change.

openQuestions.title: Cite the WICG shared-storage interestGroups() PR or explainer section directly
openQuestions.detail: The 'see also' links the explainer root; deep-link to the specific section that defines interestGroups() inside the worklet so the spec match is one click away.

summary: Solid baseline that actually invokes the API surface and degrades to a probe when Privacy Sandbox flags are off, but the worklet's return value never reaches the page, which dulls the demonstration. Pipe the IG list out of the worklet, split the failure modes, and the page lands.
